description: Add trading-hour window to SSS: restrict entries to 08:00â€“20:00 UTC. The SHAP analysis shows hour_cos (0.345) and hour_sin (0.240) are the top-2 features by a wide margin, and the decoded actionable rule (hour_sin<0.75 AND hour_cos<0.63) maps to roughly 09â€“21 UTC â€” the London+NY liquidity window. Gold is a macro asset that trends during institutional hours; during Asian session, XAU/USD mean-reverts and SSS breakout entries get chopped up. Currently SSS has zero hour filtering, meaning it generates signals during low-liquidity overnight hours that drag win rate down. The asian_breakout strategy already uses london_entry_start/end_utc â€” SSS should too.
expected_improvement: The weak-filter rule shows 36.2% win rate vs 30.3% baseline (1.20x lift, n=80) when filtering by hour. Adding a clean hour window should capture most of this lift â€” expect +4-6pp win rate improvement. This is the single highest-impact change available since time features dominate the SHAP ranking.
